content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom N:17 clearly visible in the center. Looking at the image, I can confirm that atom index 17 is indeed nitrogen (N), and I can see its covalent connections.

From the image, atom N:17 is bonded to:
- C:18 (carbon with index 18) - connected via a blue bond
- C:20 (carbon with index 20) - connected via a blue bond  
- H:48 (hydrogen with index 48) - connected via a white/gray bond

These three atoms are directly covalently bonded to N:17, forming a trigonal pyramidal geometry around the nitrogen atom.

The geomconf_index can be determined from the file name "geomconf_6180.xyz", which indicates geomconf_index = 6180.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom index 17 (N) are:
- Atom index 18, element C
- Atom index 20, element C
- Atom index 48, element H

And the geomconf_index is 6180.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 17, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
